data.body:
Goal:
Make Knots-generated workflow instructions use `kno rollback` for non-happy-path exits so agents are not given conflicting failure-path commands. Failure and request-changes paths should describe one rollback-based contract instead of mixing `kno next` with manual state mutation.

Verification:
1. Inspect the relevant claim or skill prompt for an agent-owned review step with a reject path; expected result: the failure path tells the agent to use `kno rollback <id> --actor-kind agent --agent-name <AGENT_NAME> --agent-model <AGENT_MODEL> --agent-version <AGENT_VERSION>`.
2. Inspect the corresponding happy-path instructions; expected result: they still use `kno next ...` and do not regress.
3. Add and run targeted tests for the emitted prompt or skill text; expected result: failure paths use rollback syntax and no longer instruct agents to mutate state manually.

Constraints:
- Remove ambiguity between claim-boundary text and workflow skill text for reject paths.
- Standardize on `kno rollback` rather than `kno update --status ...` for step failure exits.
data.title: Use rollback commands in failure-path prompts
